verses.1: Better is the poor who walks in his integrity
verses.2: It isn’t good to have zeal without knowledge,
verses.3: The foolishness of man subverts his way;
verses.5: A false witness shall not be unpunished.
verses.6: Many will entreat the favor of a ruler,
verses.7: All the relatives of the poor shun him;
verses.8: He who gets wisdom loves his own soul.
verses.9: A false witness shall not be unpunished.
verses.10: Delicate living is not appropriate for a fool,
verses.11: The discretion of a man makes him slow to anger.
verses.12: The king’s wrath is like the roaring of a lion,
verses.13: A foolish son is the calamity of his father.
verses.14: House and riches are an inheritance from fathers,
verses.15: Slothfulness casts into a deep sleep.
verses.16: He who keeps the commandment keeps his soul,
verses.17: He who has pity on the poor lends to the LORD;
verses.18: Discipline your son, for there is hope;
verses.19: A hot-tempered man must pay the penalty,
verses.20: Listen to counsel and receive instruction,
verses.21: There are many plans in a man’s heart,
verses.22: That which makes a man to be desired is his kindness.
verses.23: The fear of the LORD leads to life, then contentment;
verses.24: The sluggard buries his hand in the dish;
verses.25: Flog a scoffer, and the simple will learn prudence;
verses.26: He who robs his father and drives away his mother
verses.27: If you stop listening to instruction, my son,
